_comment: Shared predator lookup. Each predator has a name, a brief description for atmospheric rendering, and an array of region IDs where it is appropriate. The app selects a predator by filtering to the current region and rolling. If no regionally-appropriate predators exist, fall back to the generic set. Stat blocks are deliberately omitted — GMs should refer to the relevant Free League supplement for combat mechanics.

predators.name: Brown bear
predators.description: A lone brown bear — huge, unhurried, dangerous only when threatened or guarding cubs.

predators.name: Wild boar
predators.description: A tusked boar — territorial, bad-tempered, swift over short distances. A charge is a serious matter.

predators.name: Wolf pack
predators.description: A pack of grey wolves — lean with winter, coordinated, intelligent. They rarely attack without reason, but reasons in hard times are plentiful.

predators.name: Lone wolf
predators.description: A single wolf — outcast from its pack, or a scout for one. Wary but desperate.

predators.name: Warg
predators.description: A warg — larger than any wolf, yellow-eyed, intelligent beyond animal reason. Where one walks, others may follow.

predators.name: Warg pack
predators.description: A pack of wargs — hunting together with terrible purpose, perhaps bearing orc-riders.

predators.name: Crebain
predators.description: A flock of great black crows — unnaturally attentive, circling, reporting to some watcher in the east.

predators.name: Great eagle
predators.description: An eagle of the Misty Mountains — vast-winged, piercing of eye, not an enemy but no friend to be presumed upon.
predators.stat_reference: Core rulebook — adversary statistics; a great eagle is as likely a saviour as a peril

predators.name: Attercop
predators.description: A great spider — eight-legged, quick, venomous, with eyes that shine like cold lamps in the dim.

predators.name: Attercop brood
predators.description: Several great spiders hunting as one — webs strung between trunks, prey strung between webs.

predators.name: Mountain cat
predators.description: A great tawny cat of the high places — silent on the stones, patient, a single spring from concealment.

predators.name: Pack of feral dogs
predators.description: Dogs gone wild, turned on by hunger and lost pack-order — more dangerous than wolves in some ways, for they do not fear men.

predators.name: A beast from the deep
predators.description: Something surfacing off the Lindon coast — long and dark, more serpent than fish, ancient eyes.
predators.stat_reference: GM discretion — no canonical stat block; treat as unique encounter

predators.name: Cave-beast
predators.description: A creature of the deep places — blind, pale, toothed. What it is exactly, none can say.
predators.stat_reference: GM discretion — compose from core adversary building rules

predators.name: Marsh-dweller
predators.description: A thing of the wet places — slick-skinned, unpleasantly humanoid, all teeth and grip.
predators.stat_reference: GM discretion — compose from core adversary building rules
